_comment: Pre-computed outputs for Lab 5. Use if Ollama unavailable.

merge_intervals_v1.prompt_summary: Basic: implement merge_intervals with spec + examples
merge_intervals_v1.note: This is a common correct solution. The model often gets this right on first try for well-specified prompts.

merge_intervals_v1_weak.note: Without sorting first, chained overlaps like [[1,3],[2,5],[4,8]] fail — merges first two but misses the chain.

find_second_largest_tests.note: Model typically generates 5-7 tests. Common omissions: single element list, very large numbers, mixed positive/negative.

refactored_find_second_largest.note: O(n) single-pass. Common model error: forgetting n != first check, causing duplicates to update second.

refactored_format_task_summary.code:
def format_task_summary(task: dict) -> str:
    priority = task.get('priority', 'medium').upper()
    title = task.get('title', 'Untitled Task')
    assignee = task.get('assignee', 'Unassigned')
    status = task.get('status', 'open')
    due_date = task.get('due_date')

    lines = [
        f'[{priority}] {title}',
        f'Assigned to: {assignee}',
        f'Status: {status}',
    ]
    if due_date is not None:
        lines.append(f'Due: {due_date}')

    return '\n'.join(lines)
